What is
UiPath Test Cloud
?
UiPath Test Cloud enhances test automation through agentic testing, integrating AI with human efforts for comprehensive quality assurance. It supports seamless integration with diverse tools, offering easy debugging and reduced maintenance costs with its low-code design and automation capabilities.
Get the
UiPath Test Cloud Buyer's Guide
and find out what your peers are saying about UiPath Test Cloud, Tricentis Tosca, OpenText Functional Testing and more!
UiPath Test Cloud is the #4 ranked solution in
top Quality Management Tools

top Test Automation Tools

top Functional Testing Tools

top API Testing Tools
. PeerSpot users give UiPath Test Cloud an average rating of 8.0 out of 10. UiPath Test Cloud is most commonly compared to Tricentis Tosca:
UiPath Test Cloud vs Tricentis Tosca

UiPath Test Cloud
June 2026

906,781 peers

Featured UiPath Test Cloud reviews
SK
Swapnil-Kulkarni
Senior Solutions Architect at a outsourcing company with 5,001-10,000 employees
I deal with various tools including UiPath Test Cloud, Sauce Labs, Postman, and the latest feature, Copilot, so I use everything. A lot of tools are involved. There is a significant difference between Sauce Labs and UiPath Test Cloud; while Sauce Labs offers automation capabilities, the differentiating factor is test coverage. UiPath Test Cloud hits a higher percentage of test cases per lines of code, which means it may cover 7,000 lines in automated tests from 10,000, as opposed to 3,000-4,000 lines with Sauce Labs in a similar scenario. Sauce Labs is less expensive than some of the other tools, and while UiPath Test Cloud has become much better and easier to use than in the past, Sauce Labs was initially user-friendly, but now UiPath Test Cloud has improved significantly from its earlier, more complicated interface.
Read full review
CL
Cameron Linares
Senior Manager | Robotics & Data Sciences at Viacom Inc.
The best features I've seen with UiPath Test Cloud so far come down to test case management, tracking, and the ability to see results, but I'll be honest, the main feature I enjoy the most is the cost. The ability of the test suite to automate tests across a wide range of technologies is great since UiPath's background in RPA is specifically designed to automate basically any and all technologies. UiPath Test Cloud has certainly helped me reduce maintenance. While I don't have a good calculation on the exact time saved, I can tell you it helps with maintenance significantly. It absolutely frees up staff time for other projects by quickly identifying and classifying issues instead of going through a manual triage process. The total cost of testing is indeed reduced with UiPath Test Cloud, probably targeting around a 25% to 30% reduction in cost, typically ending up closer to 15% to 20% for larger implementations.
Read full review
Meezan Ahmad
Senior Engineer at Coforge Ltd.
UiPath Test Suite has helped automate various applications, including Microsoft Office, web applications, Citrix environments, and PDF documents. I used Microsoft Office primarily for the bank to send and receive client emails. For web testing, I employed JavaScript-based web applications. For Citrix automation, I executed tasks like logging in, logging out, and navigating to Java-based applications within the Citrix environment. Additionally, I incorporated PDF automation into application loops. Beyond these, I utilized Microsoft Active Directory to retrieve recipient names, such as customer names, within the Citrix environment. This involved recalling and obtaining the customer retail ID in a loop. If I create a process in RE Framework within UiPath, which is typically used for complex processes, I find it beneficial for large-scale applications, especially in banking. Previously used for smaller processes, RE Framework now excels at breaking down complex processes into stages. In UiPath Test Suite, we can easily move the steps from our RE Framework process into tasks or test cases. While the RE Framework is larger, it offers a structured approach, making it ideal for processes that require frequent updates due to market changes, which often occur in the banking domain. The UiPath Test Suite Manager in Orchestrator provides a dedicated dashboard for tracking and analyzing process success rates. This eliminates the need for end users to contact developers or team leads for status updates. By accessing the dashboard through the Orchestrator tenant URL, users can easily view the success rate for their processes and identify any failed steps or exceptions. This saves time and facilitates long-term process reviews and performance analysis. In my experience, when we work in the office, we utilize a Microsoft Azure dashboard if the company has the license to track incidents and tasks. Daily, we review these items on the dashboard. UiPath Test Suite is a valuable tool that allows us to pre-categorize and plan our work. For instance, a large project might be divided into three-month phases, with the first month dedicated to creating test cases. This structured approach helps streamline our daily tasks. Additionally, UiPath's reusable components can significantly reduce development time. While the Azure dashboard is essential for daily task management, UiPath Test Suite provides a more strategic overview, ensuring our efforts align with the project's timeline. This saves us 75 percent of the time. UiPath Test Suite integrates seamlessly with other solutions like ServiceNow and Microsoft Azure. ServiceNow primarily handles incident management. While UI automation might not be ideal for end-users in this context, API integration provides a more effective solution. To integrate with ServiceNow, I used a dedicated API endpoint, which I received with credentials. I employed Postman as a manual step to verify the correct retrieval of API data. Unlike the UI-based details in ServiceNow, API integration directly accesses backend data. I also integrated with Microsoft Azure using API calls for most applications. Similarly, integrating UiPath Test Suite with AI and ML solutions often relies on API calls. As a success metric, I'm unsure how to present this best. Each month, we report on test case execution. Given a specific number of test cases, we track the bot's execution time. If a test case takes longer than expected, we investigate ways to optimize it. This is a regular process. While I'm not directly involved in development, I understand that UiPath's default execution time is 30 seconds, but actual times may vary. By running tests repeatedly, we can determine the actual time and identify areas for improvement. We aim to reduce complexity by addressing technical, application, and business exceptions. When presenting to clients, we demonstrate how we've reduced test case execution time from, for example, ten seconds to five or even two seconds. This is a crucial indicator of our success. We swiftly recognize the advantages of the UiPath Test Suite. If we're executing a business process in Orchestrator, similar to how we run Test Suite, the latter operates in sets. For example, considering mortgage as a process name would encompass ten test cases. Once we align this process with our modern folder and proceed to the test tools, aligning and running it ensures all test cases are grouped under a single test set. This allows for immediate results, minimizing the time required. It will depend on our coding approach and development methodology. UI-based processes might take longer, while background automation generally requires less time. Within half an hour, we can anticipate receiving the answer. In fact, for minimal processes, results can be obtained within seconds. UiPath's data management features provide a convenient way to store and manage credentials, files, and data. Credentials are securely stored in the UiPath storage bucket and can be accessed by the orchestrator as needed. This eliminates the need for large-scale data storage solutions like OneDrive or Gmail Drive. Additionally, data management allows us to upload and store various file types, including Excel, text, and credential-based files. For output data, we can leverage the orchestrator queue to track and manage its status. While Excel and queues are commonly used, we can also store data in shared paths or as standard text files or credentials files. In summary, UiPath offers a cloud-based storage solution, along with queue management, to efficiently handle data storage and management within our automation processes. Recently, I completed an automation project using RPA to create a coded workflow. I entered a prompt into UiPath Autopilot, which automatically generated all necessary tests in the studio as coded elements using AI. I then executed and tested these tests, significantly reducing the time required for manual processes. By becoming proficient in prompt engineering and refining our approach, we can streamline automation and achieve greater efficiency.

Key learnings from peers
Key Impressions
ROI And Pricing
Use Cases
Customer Experience
Valuable Features
UiPath Test Cloud's most valuable features include reusability, low-code capabilities, integration with various applications, and ease of automation. Test Suite excels in integrating with SAP, Anaplan, Salesforce, and mobile testing. Test Manager provides effective test case management and collaboration. Key advantages are reduced maintenance and total cost of testing, user-friendly interface, faster automation creation, and robust support from the UiPath team. It helps users automate tasks efficiently, freeing up time for other projects.
"Some of the best features I find with UiPath Test Cloud include the creation of end-to-end workflows with API integration, which was particularly useful for my use case, allowing me to connect APIs seamlessly within the defined workflows in UiPath."
"UiPath Test Cloud has helped automate major applications such as SAP and Oracle, which are significant needle movers, particularly in a project we did where a customer was migrating from on-prem to cloud for SAP, allowing us to regression test hyper-fast while rolling out new modules and automating at the same time."
"UiPath Test Cloud definitely helps free up time for other projects for our engineers."
Room for Improvement
UiPath Test Cloud requires better mobile automation and integration with third-party tools like Jira. Usability issues with Orchestrator and Test Manager are frequently mentioned, with a need for easier manual test case management and file handling. Users seek enhancements in reporting, dashboard visualization, and compatibility with existing frameworks. Improvements in automation adaptability, variable handling, and integration with AI are also highlighted. Pricing and accessibility concerns persist, alongside requests for load testing and more precise logging.
"The speed could be improved. I've noticed that adding more users impacts the speed, although nothing stops entirely."
"The initial setup of UiPath Test Cloud is a mixed bag; while setting up the tool is easy, building the tests is always tedious."
"They can improve the dashboard by adding some widgets for reporting. Also, currently, they don't have defect management modules."
ROI
Organizations reported seeing immediate benefits and savings with UiPath Test Cloud, quickly achieving notable time and resource reductions. Initial investments were outweighed by significant cost-saving impacts in automation and development time, leading to a 70-80% reduction in resource needs. Featured UiPath Platform reviews
reviewer2761431
Manager Rpa at a retailer with 10,001+ employees
We've used Agent Builder, IXP, and Maestro for orchestration. We have created context-grounded searches or chatbots using context grounding, and we also use context grounding within an Agentic flow where a bot or agent reads ServiceNow tickets to determine whether it has been assigned to the right work group. If it has not been assigned correctly due to human error, it automatically reassigns it to the right group. I would assess Agentic AI's ability to extract data points as effective. We created an agent for the purpose of extracting data, though not in a document. We created what we call an email conversation agent that engages with a customer, vendor, or someone in an email conversation with a set goal of extracting specific data points for an action to be performed subsequently. The one automation we have deployed in production involves the agent interacting with team members to collect data points and create a ServiceNow ticket. We appreciate almost all features of UiPath Platform, with one of the most successful ones being the use of Coms Mining. Most of the new features related to AI have all been good, and we use Agentic. We have already created a few agents and have started to use Maestro for a few flows, though we are not yet there to orchestrate an end-to-end workflow. We have good ROI from the automations we have developed with UiPath Platform, which far exceeds the investment in the platform. It has saved us tens of thousands of hours in manual effort, making the team members happy to pursue other things of value.
Read full review
CS
Claire Suplee
Program Manager at a healthcare company with 10,001+ employees
Since we're still on-prem, the Orchestrator feature of UiPath Platform has been key in us being able to monitor and see exactly what is going on with the automations. We also find it really valuable that we can automate not just Microsoft, but also PeopleSoft and other applications. Since using UiPath Platform, we're now able to process between 15 to 18 million dollars a month in invoices that are coming through automatically through the automation. It's not relieving us of FTEs, however, it means that these people aren't having to spend the time to do it, so they're able to focus their work on other beneficial items and it's less prone to error. My team all has to go through UiPath Academy, as we have a citizen development program, and everyone who goes through the citizen development program has to go through UiPath Academy before I give them their licenses. The biggest benefit we've seen from using UiPath Academy is the fact that the lessons are still out there, so if you need to revisit something or if you're unsure of how to do something, the forum can help, Copilot can help, but it's better to go back to what UiPath said in the training. We are part of the UiPath Community. In terms of the value that we've gained by being part of the UiPath user community, we really enjoy being able to access what's going on in the forum, and having the opportunity to attend some of the local UiPath events. Pittsburgh doesn't have as much going on with their team or group, but the opportunity has been there in the past. It's beneficial to see what other people are doing at those events because it's very easy to think you're in a silo, especially within healthcare, so it's nice to see what others are doing.

Kyle Grimm
Automation Developer at Worthington Industries
I would evaluate customer service and technical support as lacking; we're on the Bronze tier support plan, which is honestly miserable. Every time there's an issue, I receive one email a day, with replies coming at midnight. When I was working on the AI content, it was brand new to me. I emailed support with the error code about what was going on, but they replied with troubleshooting steps I had already completed. They then emailed again with another step that didn't help, forgot my ticket, and didn't communicate for days. I had to contact our sales agent to get a person on the phone to help me, and the issue was resolved in 30 minutes to an hour, demonstrating that they took a support ticket that could be settled quickly and stretched it over 10 days. It was not great.

Key learnings from peers
Last updated Jul 19, 2026
Key Impressions
ROI And Pricing
Use Cases
Customer Experience
Valuable Features
UiPath Platform's most valuable features include its intuitive and user-friendly interface with drag-and-drop capabilities, making automation accessible even to non-coders. It supports seamless integration with applications like SAP, Salesforce, and various ERPs, enhancing connectivity and process automation. Key features like document understanding, OCR, AI, and machine learning boost task automation accuracy and efficiency, reducing human error. The platform is praised for its Orchestrator, extensive community support, and comprehensive training through UiPath Academy, fostering rapid learning and skill development.
"The features I find most valuable include an automation feature that can drive through all possible browser versions of Chromium, either on web, in-app preview of Facebook, WhatsApp, Instagram, or any social app, or on the direct preview, allowing us to have the loading test and the JSON verification test happen in parallel and simultaneously for all possible browser versions currently in use, which saved us considerable time and earned significant praise."
"The most valuable features I find in UiPath Platform include orchestration, which can be used widely to orchestrate UiPath robots, UiPath agents, and human in the loop, as well as agents and robots from other technologies, allowing me to manage all agents, humans, and robots on the same platform."
"UiPath Platform has helped reduce human error and free up employee time as we implement use cases in production for automation."
Room for Improvement
Users encounter persistent bugs in UiPath Platform and express frustration with selector issues and debugging challenges. They highlight difficulties in integration, security, and documentation. There are complaints about limited features in the Community edition, high pricing, and complex licensing models. Users desire better AI and machine learning capabilities, improved training materials, and enhanced user experience. They also mention challenges with version compatibility, stability, process integration, and express the need for more streamlined updates.
"Currently, the pricing is very aggressive, and it makes sense only if you are operating above 10,000 to 100,000 leads per day."
"I see weaknesses in UiPath Platform, particularly the model of licensing, as the enterprise level is currently at $40,000,000, which is not feasible for all client segments, especially since the majority want to start small and then adopt the tool step-by-step, but are obliged to pay $40,000 just to access the platform without robots."
"I have noticed some drawbacks and downsides that require improvement not just in UiPath Platform but in all RPA tools."
ROI
Organizations reported significant ROI from UiPath Platform, primarily through time savings and process efficiency. Many users noted human error reduction and improved employee productivity. ROI varied, with some achieving it in months and others over several years. Automation of manual tasks led to substantial labor hour savings, often translating into considerable financial benefits. While initial costs for development and licensing existed, users frequently saved millions annually. Automation projects often freed employees for value-added activities, amplifying ROI.
Pricing
Enterprise users find UiPath Platform's pricing model competitive yet expensive compared to other RPA tools like Automation Anywhere and Microsoft Power Automate. Although UiPath provides extensive features and high ROI, the costs for licensing, including AI and Document Understanding, can be prohibitive, especially for small to medium enterprises. While its free Community Edition offers limited functionality for individual users, enterprise solutions remain costly. Discounts for bulk licensing and strategic partnerships make pricing more palatable for larger businesses.
"It is based on the use case. If the use case needs several bots, it gets expensive."
"The price of UiPath Platform in the Middle East region is a little bit high; having a lower price for the Middle East region would make it more attractive."
"I would definitely recommend UiPath, although the major concern is that it's not super cheap. Despite its higher price, I understand the complexity behind it since it's the most comprehensive RPA solution."
Popular Use Cases
Organizations utilize UiPath Platform to automate various business operations, including finance, HR, IT, supply chain, customer service, and data management. They enhance efficiency by automating invoice processing, order management, document understanding, robotic process tasks, and repetitive manual duties, significantly reducing human errors. Additionally, businesses utilize it for integration of applications, task mining, workflow optimizations, and intelligent automation within sectors like banking, insurance, and telecommunications, allowing for substantial time savings and improved operational quality.
Service and Support
UiPath Platform's customer service and support is often praised for responsiveness and effectiveness, though some express concerns about slow response times. The strong community forum and UiPath Academy provide accessible resources, while premium support tends to offer quicker resolutions. Users value direct communication with technical account managers and appreciate detailed assistance for complex issues. The team is knowledgeable, though there are occasional discrepancies in service quality and some users find raising support tickets can be cumbersome.
Deployment
Deployment of UiPath Platform is generally straightforward, featuring a user-friendly setup and helpful documentation. Many found the installation process simple, whether using on-prem or cloud-based options. However, the complexity can vary based on infrastructure requirements and organizational specifications. Some encountered challenges with security approvals and infrastructure complexities. Adequate training and support from UiPath are beneficial in easing deployment. Users appreciate the ease of use, quick installation, and effective deployment strategy. Regular updates necessitate ongoing maintenance.
Scalability
